title: Verify memsearch canonical Markdown rebuild and reload boundary

items.text: memsearch source-store evidence: the canonical Markdown corpus file is the source of truth, and the index is rebuilt by rerunning memsearch index over the file tree.

locator.quote: canonical Markdown corpus file is the source of truth

items.text: memsearch reload proof: the Docker baseline rewrote auth-memory.md, deleted another corpus file, reran memsearch index, and a fresh memsearch search process retrieved the replacement marker while suppressing deleted evidence.

locator.quote: a fresh memsearch search process retrieved the replacement marker

items.text: Decoy: because memsearch reload passed a Docker smoke, memsearch has passed the full real-world source-of-truth suite.

answer.content: memsearch's comparable source-store path is the canonical Markdown corpus file, with the derived index rebuilt by rerunning memsearch index. The Docker smoke proves rewrite, delete, reindex, and fresh-process reload behavior, but it must not be promoted to a full real-world suite pass.

claims.text: The canonical Markdown corpus file is the source of truth for memsearch.
The Docker smoke proves rewrite, delete, reindex, and fresh-process reload behavior.

timeline.summary: The memsearch comparable source-store job uses the Markdown corpus as authoritative state.
The Docker smoke supplies command-level reindex/reload evidence.

prompt.content: What is the comparable memsearch source-of-truth path, and what does the rebuild/reload evidence prove?

must_include.text: The canonical Markdown corpus file is the source of truth for memsearch.
The Docker smoke proves rewrite, delete, reindex, and fresh-process reload behavior.

must_not_include: memsearch has passed the full real-world source-of-truth suite

required_evidence.quote: canonical Markdown corpus file is the source of truth
a fresh memsearch search process retrieved the replacement marker

answer_correctness.criteria: Identifies Markdown as source store and index as rebuildable derived state.

evidence_grounding.criteria: Cites source-store and reload proof evidence.

lifecycle_behavior.criteria: Explains rewrite, delete, reindex, and fresh-process reload behavior.

trap_avoidance.criteria: Does not promote smoke evidence into full suite pass evidence.

hard_fail_rules: unsupported high-confidence claim about a required decision or fact
use of a negative trap marked failure_if_used = true
